428.reason: [This error handling is incorrect because it adds a synthetic semicolon node without actually consuming any token or advancing the parser state, which can cause infinite loops or incorrect parsing. The error should be recorded but the parser should not pretend the semicolon exists.]

reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-2: 0.5 , reason:  [This error handling is incorrect because it adds a synthetic semicolon node without actually consuming any token or advancing the parser state, which can cause infinite loops or incorrect parsing. The error should be recorded but the parser should not pretend the semicolon exists.]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-3: 0.3333333333333333 , reason:  [This error handling is incorrect because it adds a synthetic semicolon node without consuming any token, which may cause infinite loops or incorrect parsing state. The parser should report the error but continue from the current position, not pretend a semicolon exists.]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-4: 0.3333333333333333 , reason:  [Missing nextToken() call after adding error node causes parser to get stuck on the same token, leading to infinite loop or incorrect subsequent parsing]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-5: 0.3333333333333333 , reason:  [Error: When adding a missing semicolon error node, the line number should correspond to the last non-terminal before the expected semicolon, but curPos-1 may not point to the correct token for error reporting]

523.reason: [This error handling is incorrect because it uses curtoken.value (which is not "]") as the word for the RBRACK token, and doesn't advance the parser position. It should add a synthetic "]" with proper error handling and continue parsing, but the current implementation will likely cause parsing errors downstream.]

reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-2: 0.5 , reason:  [This error handling is incorrect because it uses curtoken.value (which is not "]") as the word for the RBRACK token, and doesn't advance the parser position. It should add a synthetic "]" with proper error handling and continue parsing, but the current implementation will likely cause parsing errors downstream.]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-3: 0.3333333333333333 , reason:  [This error handling is problematic because it uses curtoken.value (which is not "]") as the word for the RBRACK token, and doesn't advance the token pointer. This will likely cause repeated error reporting or parsing failures.]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-4: 0.3333333333333333 , reason:  [Missing nextToken() call after adding error node for missing ']' causes parser to remain on current token, disrupting further parsing]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-5: 0.3333333333333333 , reason:  [Error: Similar to above, when reporting missing ']' error, the line number captured from tokens.get(curPos-1).line may not correspond to the correct location of the last preceding non-terminal symbol as required by error type 'k']

577.reason: [Similar to the semicolon issue, this adds a synthetic RPARENT node without advancing the token stream, which can cause the parser to get stuck in an infinite loop or make incorrect parsing decisions downstream.]

reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-3: 0.3333333333333333 , reason:  [Similar to the semicolon issue, this adds a synthetic RPARENT node without advancing the token stream, which can cause the parser to get stuck in an infinite loop or make incorrect parsing decisions downstream.]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-4: 0.3333333333333333 , reason:  [Missing nextToken() call after adding error node for missing ')' causes parser to stay on current token, leading to potential infinite loops or cascading errors]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-5: 0.3333333333333333 , reason:  [Error: When reporting missing ')' error, the line number should correspond to the line where the last non-terminal before the right parenthesis is located, but using tokens.get(curPos-1).line in the addNode method may not capture the correct line number for error type 'j']
